LABEL: Airway compromise
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Call 911 if stridor present; Do not lay patient flat; NPO
RATIONALE: Stridor or inability to swallow saliva suggests epiglottitis or deep space infection

LABEL: Peritonsillar abscess signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent ENT evaluation; CT neck with contrast; IV antibiotics
RATIONALE: Muffled voice + trismus strongly suggests peritonsillar abscess

LABEL: Significant neck swelling
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent evaluation for deep space infection; CT neck
RATIONALE: Neck swelling with fever suggests deep space infection

LABEL: Respiratory distress
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Assess oxygen saturation; Consider chest X-ray
RATIONALE: Dyspnea with pharyngitis needs workup

LABEL: Immunocompromised patient
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Lower threshold for testing and antibiotics; Consider broader differential
RATIONALE: Immunocompromised patients need more aggressive workup

LABEL: Persistent symptoms > 10 days
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Consider mono test; CBC; Consider ENT referral
RATIONALE: Persistent pharyngitis beyond viral window needs evaluation

LABEL: Possible scarlet fever
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Rapid strep test; Consider scarlet fever treatment
RATIONALE: Rash + fever + sore throat = possible scarlet fever

LABEL: Mastoiditis signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent CT temporal bone; IV antibiotics; ENT consult stat
RATIONALE: Mastoid tenderness + fever suggests acute mastoiditis requiring emergent evaluation

LABEL: Mastoid pain with severe symptoms
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent ENT evaluation; CT temporal bone; Pain management
RATIONALE: Severe mastoid pain without fever still requires urgent workup

LABEL: Facial nerve involvement
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent ENT evaluation; CT/MRI temporal bone; Consider Ramsay Hunt syndrome; Start antivirals if vesicles present
RATIONALE: Facial weakness with ear pain suggests facial nerve involvement — emergent evaluation required

LABEL: Neurological symptoms
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Urgent audiogram; MRI IAC; Neurology consult
RATIONALE: Vertigo/sudden hearing loss with ear pain suggests inner ear or central pathology

LABEL: Ear trauma with drainage
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Keep ear dry; ENT evaluation within 24h; Do not place anything in ear canal
RATIONALE: Trauma with drainage may indicate TM perforation

LABEL: Ear trauma with neuro signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT temporal bone; Neurology consult; Urgent ENT
RATIONALE: Trauma + neuro symptoms suggests temporal bone fracture

LABEL: Neck swelling with fever
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT neck with contrast; Consider deep space infection; ENT evaluation
RATIONALE: Neck swelling with fever and ear pain suggests parotid/deep space infection

LABEL: Immunocompromised patient
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Lower threshold for imaging and antibiotics; Consider malignant otitis externa
RATIONALE: Immunocompromised patients with ear pain need broader workup

LABEL: PE risk - SOB + chest pain
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT pulmonary angiography stat; D-dimer; Oxygen monitoring; Anticoagulation if confirmed
RATIONALE: SOB + chest pain with cough raises high suspicion for pulmonary embolism

LABEL: PE risk - chest pain + DVT signs
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT pulmonary angiography; Lower extremity duplex ultrasound; D-dimer; Anticoagulation if confirmed
RATIONALE: Chest pain with DVT risk factors (leg swelling or recent immobility/surgery) requires emergent PE workup

LABEL: Chest pain with cough
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Chest X-ray; ECG; Consider troponin; Outpatient CTA if Wells score elevated
RATIONALE: Chest pain with persistent cough needs urgent cardiac and pulmonary evaluation

LABEL: Hemoptysis
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: CT chest with contrast; CBC; Coagulation studies; ENT/Pulm consult
RATIONALE: Hemoptysis requires emergent evaluation to rule out PE, malignancy, or pulmonary hemorrhage

LABEL: Hypoxemia
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Supplemental oxygen; ABG; Chest X-ray; CT if needed; Admit for monitoring
RATIONALE: Low oxygen saturation with cough requires emergent evaluation and oxygen support

LABEL: SOB without chest pain
IMMEDIATE_ACTIONS: Chest X-ray; PFTs; Consider CT chest; Pulse oximetry
RATIONALE: Shortness of breath with persistent cough warrants urgent pulmonary evaluation
